portfolio.title: Studies & Training
portfolio.short: Foundational education underlying the current work.
portfolio.full: Details on degree program and training stages will be added here soon.

portfolio.title: Building a Career as a Lecturer
portfolio.short: Developing independent speaking and teaching work.
portfolio.full: For several years I have been building independent work as a lecturer and speaker – from first individual talks to regular teaching assignments.
portfolio.approach: The path led from volunteer talks to paid assignments with educational institutions, associations and companies.

portfolio.title: Board Work at the Deafblind Association
portfolio.short: Representing deafblind people at the association level.
portfolio.full: As a board member, I represent the interests of deafblind people to policymakers, funding bodies and the public.
portfolio.approach: This includes position papers, talks with ministries and following legislation on participation.

portfolio.title: Work with the European Deafblind Union
portfolio.full: Contributing to a European working group on standards and recommendations for assistive technology for deafblind people.
portfolio.approach: The working group brings together representatives from several European countries to develop shared minimum standards.

portfolio.title: Accessible Digitalization
portfolio.short: Ongoing consulting project for public bodies and companies.
portfolio.full: Consulting for public bodies and companies implementing accessible software and websites – from initial concept to screen reader testing.
portfolio.approach: I work closely with development teams and audit existing applications using my own screen reader and braille display practice.

portfolio.title: Programming as a Deafblind Person
portfolio.short: Learning software development despite a lack of accessible learning paths.
portfolio.full: I largely taught myself programming – using a screen reader and braille display, often without accessible learning materials.
portfolio.approach: This path still shapes how I design software for other deafblind users today.

termine.title: Digital Participation Today
termine.detail: A talk on the current state of digital participation in Germany, focused on public administration and education.
termine.info: Audience: professionals from administration and education. Duration: 45 minutes incl. Q&A.

termine.title: Deafblind Association Board Meeting
termine.detail: Regular board meeting covering ongoing applications, budget and upcoming association events.
termine.info: Internal to the association – attendance by arrangement with the office.

termine.title: Designing Accessible Software
termine.detail: Hands-on workshop for engineering teams: testing your own applications together with a screen reader and braille display.
termine.info: Audience: developers and product teams. Duration: 3 hours, max. 15 participants.

termine.title: Living Deafblind – A First-Hand Account
termine.detail: A personal account of everyday life, communication and self-determination as a deafblind person.
termine.info: Audience: students and the public. Duration: 60 minutes.

termine.title: EDBU Working Group Meeting
termine.detail: Two-day meeting of the European technology working group to align on shared standards.
termine.info: By invitation of the European Deafblind Union.

termine.title: Screen Reader Testing for Dev Teams
termine.detail: Hands-on workshop on systematically testing web and app interfaces with common screen readers.
termine.info: Audience: QA and engineering. Duration: full day.

library.title: AccessNav
library.desc: Navigation app with tactile and audio feedback for deafblind users.

library.title: Dual Sensory Impairment
library.desc: An article on the specific accessibility requirements involved.

library.title: Zwischentöne
library.desc: An interview series with activists from the disability rights movement.
